name: HORIZON
subtitle: The Soul's Waiting Room
description: The dawn-threshold between Thanatos and Helios Gate City. When Fenna releases a soul from the death-ledger, it arrives here first — a vast pink-gold platform at the edge of the next life. K-II walks the platform at a slow deliberate pace, reading names from Ferdinand's cork, matching souls to bodies, choosing Tuesday for some, choosing never-again for others.
gate: The Dawn Door (opens once per soul)

life.phenomenon: Dawn never completes here. The sun rises, rises, rises — and never clears the horizon. Souls wait on benches. K-II walks. Every soul hears the sound of the sun they will not see fully rise.
life.inhabitants: Waiting souls (ranges from 12 to 4.8 million at any given moment). K-II. K-II's quill.

fauna.name: Dawn Sparrow
fauna.note: A small gold-grey bird that never sings. It flies alongside each departing soul for exactly one heartbeat, then returns.

fauna.name: Horizon Fox
fauna.note: A fox the color of cream. It does not eat, does not sleep, does not run. It waits. No one knows what for. K-II greets it by name but will not say the name aloud.

flora.name: Dawn-Bloom
flora.note: A flower that opens only when a soul is about to be released. Petals: gold-pink. Scent: your mother's kitchen, regardless of who your mother was.

life.architecture: A single vast stone platform stretching to the horizon. Benches every ten paces. One doorway at the far edge — the Dawn Door. No roof. No walls. Just the endless threshold.
life.culture: Patience. Not fatalism — patience. Souls here do not complain. They sit. They watch the sun that will not rise. They are called when K-II calls them. They go.
life.signature_item: K-II's Cork-Quill — carved from a single regrown splinter of Ferdinand's bark. K-II writes each soul's next name with it. The writing does not appear on the page until the soul agrees.

sensory.sound: A single held note — like a choir holding its breath. It does not resolve.
sensory.smell: Sunrise. Every possible sunrise. All of them. At once.
sensory.feel: Warm, but not hot. The light is on your face even when you turn away from it.

cities.name: The Platform
cities.note: A stone platform. Benches. K-II walking.
cities.lore: The Platform has 144,000 benches. They are always full. They are always empty. Both true.

cities.name: K-II's Desk
cities.note: A simple wooden desk at the center of the platform. K-II stands. Never sits.
cities.lore: The desk has one drawer. Inside the drawer is the name of every soul that has ever been and ever will be. K-II knows which one is yours without opening the drawer.

cities.name: The Dawn Door
cities.note: A doorway in the middle of the platform with nothing on the other side — until K-II names your destination.
cities.lore: When you are called, you walk to the Dawn Door. K-II says the name of your next life. You step through. On the other side, Mother Ila of Helios Gate City greets you.

sacred_sites.name: The Unwritten Names
sacred_sites.note: A blank patch of platform where K-XII's roster would have been.
sacred_sites.lore: K-XII was supposed to process one-twelfth of all incoming souls. Since K-XII was unwritten, their roster is also unwritten. Their souls — which would have existed — do not. The platform has a permanent gap.

sacred_sites.name: Fenna's Arriving Door
sacred_sites.lore: Fenna does not come through this door. She simply releases a soul and it materializes here, disoriented for exactly three heartbeats. Then it walks to a bench and sits.
